Act as a Senior Product Portfolio Consultant and Tech Copywriter.
I am a Project Lead & Product Delivery Manager specializing in PropTech, IoT, and Enterprise SaaS. I currently work at Sclera (a facility management platform often white-labeled as JLL Serve), where I manage end-to-end delivery for Fortune 500 clients.
My Goal: I need to build a professional portfolio website that showcases my ability to translate complex operational challenges into scalable digital products.
My Resume Context:
Sharun Suvarna
Project Lead | Product Delivery & Agile Program Management
sharunsuvarna92@gmail.com | +91 9945941783 | linkedin.com/in/sharun-suvarna | Mangalore, India
PROFESSIONAL SUMMARY
Project Lead - Client Solutioning & Product Delivery managing end-to-end Agile lifecycles and delivering IoT, BMS, and AI-powered digital solutions for Fortune 500 clients. Expert in translating complex operational challenges into scalable digital products. Proven expertise in stakeholder management, risk mitigation, and data-driven delivery across 200+ enterprise sites. Strategic product owner and key contributor to Sclera (white-labeled as JLL Serve) - driving digital transformation, ROI optimization, and cross-functional alignment in global facility management systems.
PROFESSIONAL EXPERIENCE
Project Lead – Client Solutioning and Delivery January 2022 - Present Sclera Mangalore, India
• Spearhead end-to-end delivery of multimillion-dollar facility management solutions for Fortune 500 clients across 200+ global sites (Tech, FMCG, and public sectors).
• Own the full project lifecycle - from RFP response, InfoSec compliance, and stakeholder engagement to solution prototyping, product roadmapping, agile execution, and post-go-live support.
• Drive pre-sales and solution consulting, converting 10+ POC pilots into enterprise-wide rollouts and securing deals ranging from $40K to $2M.
• Conduct discovery workshops and translate business challenges into scalable, configurable digital products..
• Facilitate Agile ceremonies including sprint planning, backlog grooming, and UAT cycles, while continuously refining user stories and acceptance criteria in Jira and Confluence.
• Develop and maintain PRDs, project charters, and risk registers, ensuring alignment and traceability across all phases.
• Plan and define KPI frameworks and executive dashboards to showcase before-after metrics, enabling clients to measure ROI, compliance gains, and operational efficiency improvements.
• Coordinate delivery across 10+ global squads, including Product, Engineering, Design, QA, and Support, ensuring alignment, delivery consistency, and client satisfaction.
• Enable IoT/BMS and CMMS integration (Siemens, Awair, Corrigo, Maximo) to enhance automation and data accuracy.
Trainee Engineer - Communication Systems March 2021 – December 2021
Bharat Electronics Limited Bengaluru, India
• Coordinated cross-functional teams across Planning, Logistics, QA, Security, and Design to support naval communication system projects and ensured timely delivery of 4 major defense projects aligned with client and quality standards.
• Streamlined procurement and vendor management for 800+ purchase orders, improving supply chain efficiency and compliance.
Junior Mechanical Engineer July 2018 – April 2019
Ventare Engineering Doha, Qatar
• Supported the $3M+ EPIC delivery of the Shelter in Place Building at Ras Laffan, overseeing HVAC, Plumbing, Fire Safety, and Drainage systems installation in compliance with QP standards and project timelines.
• Coordinated with consultants, subcontractors, and suppliers for approvals, material management, and SPIR documentation, ensuring smooth mechanical execution and cross-functional alignment.
EDUCATION
B.E. in Mechanical Engineering | N M A M Institute of Technology | 2014 – 2018
CGPA: 9.34
KEY PROJECTS & ACHIEVEMENTS
• Intel Smart Waste Bin Optimization Project: Led client engagement, requirements analysis, and solution design for an industrial waste bin management system, delivering $500K in annual cost savings and earning industry recognition.
• Interactive Alarm Dashboard for Google (FORT): Spearheaded delivery of an alarm triage platform across 30+ sites, improving real-time incident response and stakeholder alignment.
• Intelligent Operations Center (IOC) for Procter & Gamble: Directed solution prototyping and managed delivery across Latin and North America, enhancing alarm triage efficiency by 40% and achieving $600K in indirect savings.
• GovTech Singapore – Consumable Inventory Tool: Led requirements gathering, prototyping, and implementation of a digital inventory platform, reducing manual tracking errors by 90% and optimizing consumable lifecycle management.
• AI-Powered Asset Onboarding for Real Estate Client: Oversaw requirements and prototype development for an AI-driven asset onboarding solution, improving data capture and validation efficiency by 75%.
• Reseller Portal for Internal Channel Management: Spearheading the design and deployment of an internal portal to enhance partner management, client metrics tracking, and reporting automation.
SKILLS
• Project Management: End-to-end project lifecycle management, Agile, Global deployments.
• Client Solutioning: Discovery workshops, Gap analysis, Solution design, Prototyping
• Product Delivery: Roadmapping, SDLC, Feature scoping, Release management, QA oversight
• Facility Management Expertise: Facility management systems (FMS), IoT and BMS integrations, Alarm management
• Data & Performance Metrics: KPI planning, Dashboard design, ROI tracking
• Tools & Innovation: Jira, Zoho Projects, Miro, Figma, Visily, MS Office.
• Technical: Prompt engineering, OpenAI API integration, SQL basics, AWS Cloud foundation.
CERTIFICATIONS
• Professional Scrum Master™ I (PSM I) – Scrum.org Certified in Agile-based project management, iterative product development, and cross-team collaboration.
• Prompt Engineering & Programming with OpenAI – Columbia+
Certified in prompt design, OpenAI API integration, and applying LLMs for automation and data analysis using Python libraries.
• Agile Project Management Professional Certificate – Atlassian
Certified in agile project management fundamentals, Kanban and Scrum frameworks, and Jira Software configuration for team efficiency.
• AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner – Amazon Web Services
Certified in foundational cloud concepts, AWS global infrastructure, security best practices, and core services including compute, storage, and networking.
The Product Context (Sclera): https://www.sclera.com/
Sclera is a unified platform for facility management that integrates IoT, BMS, and workflow automation to give enterprises a "single pane of glass" view of their operations. It focuses on ROI, compliance, and operational efficiency.
I need you to generate the text content for my portfolio with the following structure:
1. The "Hero" Section (Home Page):
Create a compelling Headline and Sub-headline that captures my hybrid role (Project Management + Product Solutioning).
It should sound strategic, not just administrative. Focus on "delivering value" and "digital transformation."
2. The "About Me" Narrative:
Write a professional but engaging 200-word bio.
Highlight my journey from Mechanical Engineering to managing multimillion-dollar digital transformation projects.
Emphasize my specific expertise in IoT, AI, and stakeholder management for global deployments.
3. Three Detailed Case Studies (The Core):
Please take the following project bullets from my resume and expand them into full STAR Method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) case studies.
Case Study A: The Intel Smart Waste Bin Project (Focus on the $500k savings and IoT integration).
Case Study B: The Google (FORT) Interactive Alarm Dashboard (Focus on stakeholder alignment and real-time triage).
Case Study C: P&G Intelligent Operations Center (Focus on the 40% efficiency gain and scaling across Latin/North America).
For each case study, include a section called "My Role" to specify exactly what I managed (e.g., Discovery workshops, prototyping, or agile delivery).
4. The "Methodology" Section:
Create a section that explains how I work.
Combine my Agile/Scrum certification with my "Client Solutioning" skills.
Use bullet points to describe my process: Discovery -> Solution Design -> Agile Execution -> Data-Driven Optimization.
Tone of Voice: authoritative, metric-driven, innovative, and clear.
Advice on How to Build This Portfolio
Since you are a PM, your portfolio is your product. Here is how I recommend you structure the actual layout based on your specific experience:
1. The Visuals (Crucial for B2B)
Since you can't always show proprietary client data, you need to use Artifacts to prove your skills.
Don't just show the final dashboard: Show the wireframe you made in Visily or Figma first, then the final result. This shows you understand the SDLC.
Show the Process: Take a screenshot of a generic Jira board or a Miro board you used for a discovery workshop.
Architecture: Draw a simple diagram showing how Sclera talks to the IoT sensors (Siemens/Awair). This proves technical competency.
2. The Narrative Arc
Your portfolio should tell the story of a "Bridge."
The Problem: Clients (Fortune 500s) have messy, disconnected facility data.
The Bridge (You): You translate their messy needs into Sclera’s technical capabilities.
The Result: Millions in savings and efficiency.
3. Suggested Portfolio Structure (Sitemap)
Home: Headline + "Trust Bar" (Logos of clients like Intel, Google, P&G, GovTech).
Work/Case Studies:
Project 1: IoT & Sustainability (Intel)
Project 2: Crisis Response & Dashboards (Google)
Project 3: Operational Scale (P&G)
My Toolkit: Logos of tools you use (Jira, Figma, OpenAI API, AWS, SQL). This is great for ATS scanning if you are looking for jobs.
Resume/Contact.
A Quick "Hook" for your Hero Section
If you want to start building right now, here is a strong headline based on your resume:
"I bridge the gap between complex facility operations and scalable digital products."
Delivering multimillion-dollar IoT and AI solutions for Fortune 500 enterprises through Agile leadership and data-driven strategy.

I'll likely use Framer Motion for subtle transitions, as requested. `d3` is there if needed, but I'll lean on standard UI unless it truly enhances the narrative.

I'm now mapping out the precise architecture. I'm focusing on defining the file structure, contemplating key components like the `metadata.json`, and the essential React components. I'm thinking through how to organize the main layout and routing. I’ve defined essential sections and component wrappers for consistency. The goal is to build a structure that’s scalable and easy to maintain.

I'm expanding my analysis, and incorporating specific case study data.  I've fleshed out the examples for the portfolio.  I'm now detailing each case with STAR format: Situation, Task, Action, and Result for Intel, Google, and P&G.  The goal is to showcase impact succinctly.


**Defining Core Components**

My current focus is defining the core components for the application.  I'm making a list and thinking through the structure and contents of the fundamental building blocks. I've got files defined, like `types.ts` for interfaces and `constants.tsx` for copy generation. I am also working on `Navbar`, `Hero`, `About`, `Methodology`, `CaseStudies`, `Skills`, and `Contact`. Now I'll start building the initial React components to bring them to life.


**Building the Application**

I'm now focusing on putting the components together within `App.tsx`. I'm double-checking the content generation in `constants.tsx` to ensure it precisely follows the user's prompt. I'm also ensuring the application incorporates specific Tailwind CSS styles, like background colors, text colors, shadows, and rounded corners, to provide a modern, accessible, and responsive user experience. The app will use `lucide-react` for icons and type safety via `types.ts`.


**Coding the Core Application**

I'm deep into the actual implementation, and I've focused on `components/CaseStudies.tsx`, `components/Skills.tsx`, and `components/Contact.tsx`. I've ensured a direct correlation between the user's initial instructions and the structure of `constants.tsx` to align the copywriting with the code. I've re-evaluated the case study data structure to explicitly incorporate STAR elements for clear presentation. Also, I'm integrating a Recharts bar chart to visualize quantitative results, meeting the "Data-Driven" goal.


**Constructing the Footer**

I'm presently building the footer component. I'm focusing on incorporating the copy and adding social links. The footer must have an accessible layout, so it will incorporate relevant accessibility attributes for screen readers. I'm keeping design and functionality in balance. The footer will be responsive and look good at any screen size.

The issue you are experiencing with "click functionalities not working" is likely due to a combination of factors common in this environment:

1.  **Import Resolution**: The `react-dom/client` import wasn't explicitly mapped in `index.html`, which can cause React to fail to hydrate properly in some execution environments, leaving the page unresponsive to events.
2.  **Library Compatibility**: The `recharts` library can sometimes cause runtime errors in React 19 Strict Mode or due to default prop issues, which might silently crash the component tree rendering the Case Studies section unresponsive.
3.  **Z-Index Stacking**: While the `z-index` looked mostly correct, I've increased the content z-index in the Hero section to ensure no background elements overlap interactive buttons.

I have implemented the following fixes:
*   **Updated `index.html`**: Added an explicit import map for `react-dom/client` and enabled smooth scrolling.
*   **Refactored `CaseStudies.tsx`**: Removed the heavy `recharts` dependency and replaced it with a lightweight, robust CSS/Tailwind-based bar chart. This eliminates potential JS errors crashing the section. I also ensured `cursor-pointer` is present on the selection buttons.
*   **Fixed `Hero.tsx`**: Increased the content layer z-index to `z-20` to guarantee it sits above all background effects.
*   **Fixed `Methodology.tsx`**: Corrected a potentially invalid z-index class (`-z-0` -> `z-0`).
